claim: Antigravity (Google's agent harness) uses agentic coding patterns similar to Claude Code to enable autonomous agent workflows
actionable: Study Antigravity's approach to agent harness design when building multi-step autonomous systems

claim: Browser automation combined with code execution enables agents to complete end-to-end tasks across multiple applications
actionable: Design agents that combine browser control (for user-facing tasks) with code execution (for backend logic) in a single workflow

claim: Agent reliability improves with structured error handling and tool-specific fallbacks rather than generic retry logic
actionable: Define tool-specific error handlers (e.g., if browser click fails, try alternative selector; if code fails, inspect error and rewrite)
